title: Drag-to-reorder list with finger-tracked lift and gap animation
design_brief: A Things 3 / Reminders-style reorderable list: long-press a row to lift it (rises with shadow + scale 1.04), drag to a new position, other rows animate aside to make a gap. Drop with a satisfying snap. Reference: iOS Reminders reorder, Things 3, Notion mobile list reorder.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: react-native-draggable-flatlist (heavy, opinionated)
Animating other rows via setState (re-renders the whole list)
Hard snap-back on drop (should spring)
No haptic on lift / drop
Lift activated by short press (interferes with normal tap)
mobile_reasoning: Each row owns a translateY shared value. On long-press of any row, that row enters drag mode — its z-index lifts, shadow turns on, scale springs to 1.04. As finger moves, we compute which slot the row 'belongs' to now (based on absolute y), and other rows' translateY shifts to open a gap. All positional math is in worklets — React only re-renders when the drop commits the new order.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: DragList container manages a positionsMap shared value (row id → current slot). DragRow renders with absolute position derived from positionsMap.
stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-031, define named constants for Drag-to-reorder list with finger-tracked lift and gap animation: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-031, isolate cross_platform differences for Drag-to-reorder list with finger-tracked lift and gap animation: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-031, test Drag-to-reorder list with finger-tracked lift and gap animation at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: Lift: scale + shadow opacity via spring. Other rows shift via withSpring with stagger via individual shared values keyed by id.
gesture_strategy: Gesture.LongPress(350ms) activates → followed by Gesture.Pan. Composed via Gesture.Simultaneous so the pan starts from the long-press point.
interaction_details: Long-press 350ms: row lifts with medium haptic, scales to 1.04, shadow appears
Drag: other rows shift to make gap with spring physics
Drop: selection haptic, row springs down to its new slot, onReorder fires
Tap (under 350ms): nothing (long-press required to enter drag mode)
Cancel mid-drag (gesture interrupt): row springs back to original slot
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-031 (Drag-to-reorder list with finger-tracked lift and gap animation): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps continuous gesture math for Drag-to-reorder list with finger-tracked lift and gap animation in gesture/Reanimated worklets, commits state once at release, and tests vertical-scroll coexistence plus reduced-motion fallback.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Finger-tracked lift with haptic confirmation feels like picking up a real card. 2) Other rows' gap animation is UI-thread spring physics — no jank. 3) Drop snap with spring physics is the iOS Reminders convention everyone recognizes.

title: Nested bottom sheet: detail sheet stacks above primary sheet
design_brief: A Cash App pattern where tapping a transaction in the main bottom sheet opens a detail sheet that stacks ON TOP. The primary sheet stays at its snap, the secondary slides up with a slightly higher max snap. Dismissing secondary returns control to primary without re-entering. Reference: Cash App activity → transaction, Robinhood positions → detail.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Replacing the sheet content (loses scroll position)
Pushing a new screen instead of stacking sheets
Both sheets visible but pointer events leaking to bottom one
Backdrop visible only over secondary (should dim primary too)
mobile_reasoning: Stack of sheets needs a SheetStackContext that maintains an array. Top sheet has pointerEvents:'auto', sheets beneath have pointerEvents:'none'. Backdrop opacity comes from the topmost sheet's progress. Dismissal of top sheet pops the array; primary sheet remains visible at its snap. The pattern scales to N sheets — useful for drill-down flows.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: SheetStackProvider context. useSheet hook to push/pop. Sheet component subscribes to its position in the stack.
stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-032, define named constants for Nested bottom sheet: detail sheet stacks above primary sheet: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-032, isolate cross_platform differences for Nested bottom sheet: detail sheet stacks above primary sheet: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-032, test Nested bottom sheet: detail sheet stacks above primary sheet at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: Each sheet has its own translateY. Backdrop opacity = sum of all sheet progresses, clamped to 0.6.
gesture_strategy: Each sheet has its own Pan gesture. Only the top one is active; others wait their turn.
interaction_details: Push: secondary sheet slides up to snap higher than primary
Only top sheet receives gestures (pointerEvents controlled)
Backdrop only rendered once (under deepest sheet), dims further as more sheets stack
Drag-down on top sheet dismisses it; primary returns to its original snap unchanged
Multiple levels supported (capped at maxNested for sanity)
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-032 (Nested bottom sheet: detail sheet stacks above primary sheet): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses modal/sheet primitives for Nested bottom sheet: detail sheet stacks above primary sheet; profile keyboard avoidance, snap changes, and backdrop updates while preserving modal accessibility boundaries.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Sheet stacking is a Cash App / Robinhood pattern that maps to how iOS sheets in iOS 15+ can stack natively — we get the same UX without depending on the iOS 15+ system sheet API. 2) Underlying sheets keep their scroll position because they never unmount. 3) Pointer event control is fundamental to mobile layering — no z-index trickery needed.

title: FlashList masonry grid with variable-height image cards
design_brief: A Pinterest-style masonry grid: two columns on phone, three on tablet. Cards have variable heights based on image aspect ratio. Scroll smoothly through 1000+ cards. Reference: Pinterest, Unsplash app, Mubi.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: FlatList with numColumns (forces uniform row heights — breaks masonry)
Custom masonry that re-measures all cards on each scroll
React-native-super-grid (no recycling)
Loading full-resolution images (use thumbnail variants)
mobile_reasoning: FlashList supports masonry via the masonry prop (v2+). Each item provides a height hint via overrideItemLayout — FlashList places them in the shortest column. Image decode at thumbnail size via expo-image's contentFit + low-res transformation URL prevents memory pressure on long scrolls.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: MasonryGrid wraps FlashList. Card component memoized.
stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-033, define named constants for FlashList masonry grid with variable-height image cards: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-033, isolate cross_platform differences for FlashList masonry grid with variable-height image cards: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-033, test FlashList masonry grid with variable-height image cards at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: None during scroll. Optional: fade-in per card from rn-003 pattern.
gesture_strategy: Tap card to open detail (use shared-element from rn-005).
interaction_details: Smooth scrolling at 60fps with thousands of cards
Card images load lazily; offscreen cards unload from memory
Tap card opens detail via shared-element transition
Two columns on phone, three on tablet — same code
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-033 (FlashList masonry grid with variable-height image cards): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses stable keys and virtualized row boundaries for FlashList masonry grid with variable-height image cards; profile scroll FPS, cell re-render count, and image/text measurement on a production-sized list.
what_makes_this_native: 1) True masonry recycling — Pinterest's own iOS app uses essentially this pattern with UICollectionView. 2) Per-column placement happens at insertion time, not on scroll. 3) Image cache means scroll-back is free.

title: Phone number input with country picker and live formatting
design_brief: A WhatsApp/Telegram signup phone field: country flag + dial code picker on the left, formatted phone number on the right. Live formats as user types (e.g., '4155551234' → '(415) 555-1234' for US). Country picker opens as a bottom sheet with search. Reference: WhatsApp signup, Telegram, Signal.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: react-native-phone-input (legacy, not maintained)
Country picker as a fullscreen Modal (use bottom sheet)
Formatting on every keystroke without parsing
No flag rendering on Android (emoji flags don't render on most Android)
Forgetting +1 vs +44 vs +972 dial codes
mobile_reasoning: Use libphonenumber-js for parsing — it's the reference implementation. Format as you type via AsYouType. Country picker is a bottom sheet with a sectioned list. Flags on iOS render via emoji; on Android most devices don't ship flag emojis — fallback to SVG country icons or country code as text.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: PhoneInput composes CountryButton + TextInput. CountryPicker is bottom sheet with FlashList.
stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-034, define named constants for Phone number input with country picker and live formatting: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-034, isolate cross_platform differences for Phone number input with country picker and live formatting: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-034, test Phone number input with country picker and live formatting at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: Country pick: brief spring scale on flag.
gesture_strategy: For rn-034, gestures are optional acceleration for Phone number input with country picker and live formatting; the same action must remain reachable through a labelled Pressable or accessibilityAction, with scroll-vs-pan arbitration tested on both platforms.
interaction_details: Selection haptic on country picker tap
Flag scales 1→1.2→1 spring when country changes
Live formatting per AsYouType
Submit triggers if parsed.isValid()
System keyboard shows phone-pad layout
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-034 (Phone number input with country picker and live formatting): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps typing responsive for Phone number input with country picker and live formatting by validating after input boundaries, avoiding parent list re-renders, and measuring keypress-to-paint plus keyboard open/close on device.
what_makes_this_native: 1) System phone-pad keyboard with tel autocomplete suggests SIM-stored numbers. 2) Live formatting feels like the phone is helping you. 3) Platform-aware flag rendering — iOS emoji, Android code fallback.

title: Drawer + tabs hybrid: side drawer for sections, bottom tabs for context
design_brief: A Slack-style navigation: a left drawer for switching between workspaces / sections, bottom tabs for the current section's primary screens (home, search, profile). Drawer slides over with backdrop dim; bottom tabs stay anchored. Reference: Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Drawer + tabs implemented as two separate libraries fighting each other
Drawer that resizes content instead of overlaying
Tabs that hide when drawer is open (jarring)
Drawer behind tab bar (z-order error)
mobile_reasoning: Drawer is a sibling-overlay component with its own translateX shared value, driven by edge-pan from left or by a hamburger tap. Tabs from Expo Router live below. Drawer's z-index is higher than tabs but it appears 'above' content cleanly. Backdrop renders between drawer and content.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-035, define named constants for Drawer + tabs hybrid: side drawer for sections, bottom tabs for context: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-035, isolate cross_platform differences for Drawer + tabs hybrid: side drawer for sections, bottom tabs for context: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.

Hamburger tap: selection haptic + drawer slides open
Tap scrim: drawer closes
Drag drawer past 50%: opens; below: closes
Backdrop dims to 0.5 max
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-035 (Drawer + tabs hybrid: side drawer for sections, bottom tabs for context): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Lets the native navigator own transitions for Drawer + tabs hybrid: side drawer for sections, bottom tabs for context; custom work stays in tab/screen options, with back-swipe, deep-link restore, and hidden-tab states profiled separately.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Edge-pan-to-open is the iOS convention. 2) Drawer + tabs coexisting without conflict is Slack's actual pattern. 3) iOS native blur on drawer surface.

title: Skeleton loader that mirrors the actual layout, not generic blocks
design_brief: A feed loading state: each skeleton card matches the exact dimensions and arrangement of the real card it will become. The skeleton blocks shimmer with a left-to-right gradient sweep, and individual cards animate in with stagger before content loads. Reference: Facebook iOS, LinkedIn mobile, Apple Music search loading.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: A single skeleton block per card (lazy)
ActivityIndicator centered on a blank screen
Shimmer that pauses (should be continuous loop)
Skeletons with rounded corners when the real card has square corners (mismatch on swap)
mobile_reasoning: Skeleton must MATCH the layout exactly so the swap to real content doesn't shift. Shimmer is a Skia LinearGradient that sweeps across via a repeating animation. All cards share a single shared 'shimmerProgress' value driving the gradient position — one animation engine, multiple subscribers.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: Skeleton primitives (SkelBlock, SkelCircle). FeedCardSkeleton mirrors FeedCard layout.
stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-036, define named constants for Skeleton loader that mirrors the actual layout, not generic blocks: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-036, isolate cross_platform differences for Skeleton loader that mirrors the actual layout, not generic blocks: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-036, test Skeleton loader that mirrors the actual layout, not generic blocks at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: shimmerProgress = withRepeat(withTiming(1, {duration: 1400, easing: Easing.linear}), -1, false). All SkelBlocks share this.

All skeletons in sync (shared shimmer value)
Layout EXACTLY matches real card — no shift on swap
Cards stagger-fade-in via Reanimated entering layout
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-036 (Skeleton loader that mirrors the actual layout, not generic blocks): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses UI-thread shared values and reduced-motion zero-duration paths for Skeleton loader that mirrors the actual layout, not generic blocks; profile frame time during mount/exit and assert no JS callback runs on every animation frame.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Layout-matching skeleton means zero CLS — feels like content materialized. 2) Shimmer runs on UI thread at 60fps with N skeletons sharing one engine. 3) Skia gradient is cheaper than a View with overflow-hidden + animated child.

title: Confetti burst on success using Skia particles
design_brief: On a successful action (payment sent, achievement unlocked, level complete), burst 60 colorful confetti pieces from a point on screen with physics: initial upward velocity, gravity, rotation, and fade. Pieces have varied shapes (rectangles, stars). Cleans up after 2s. Reference: Cash App money sent, Duolingo lesson complete, Strava PR.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: A library import for one-off celebration moments
Confetti via dozens of Animated.Views (jank on Android)
Confetti that lingers visually after animation done
Same color/size/rotation for every piece
mobile_reasoning: Skia is the right tool — N particles drawn in one Canvas, all positions computed in worklets. useFrameCallback updates particle positions each frame based on velocity + gravity. After 2 seconds, the component unmounts itself.

stylesheet_strategy.organization: Confetti component with imperative trigger via ref.
stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-037, define named constants for Confetti burst on success using Skia particles: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-037, isolate cross_platform differences for Confetti burst on success using Skia particles: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-037, test Confetti burst on success using Skia particles at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: useFrameCallback updates particle positions: vx const, vy += gravity * dt, rotation += angular vel. Opacity fades over last 600ms.
gesture_strategy: Touch is optional; the drawing exposes a semantic image label and avoids gesture-only meaning.
interaction_details: Burst from caller-provided point
60 pieces, varied color/size/rotation
Gravity pulls down, rotation continues
Last 30% of lifetime fades opacity
Component unmounts after 2.2s — no leftover render
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-037 (Confetti burst on success using Skia particles): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps Skia geometry for Confetti burst on success using Skia particles memoized and updates only drawing inputs; verify GPU frame time, no hook-in-loop usage, and a text summary for non-visual users.
what_makes_this_native: 1) 60 particles at 60fps — would be jank-city with 60 Animated.Views. 2) Self-cleanup avoids stuck overlays. 3) Trigger-via-ref is the imperative pattern for celebrations.

title: Photo grid picker with multi-select and recent photos preview
design_brief: An attachment picker like Instagram/iMessage: full-screen grid of recent photos (3 columns), tap to select (up to 10), selected photos get a numbered overlay (1, 2, 3...) showing send order. Reference: Instagram story picker, iMessage photo picker, Apple Mail attach.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Using expo-image-picker UI directly (no multi-select customization)
Loading full-resolution images in the grid (massive memory)
Selected state without ordering (users can't tell selection order)
Slow scroll because Image components aren't recycled
mobile_reasoning: Use expo-media-library to enumerate the device's photos with low-resolution thumbnails. Render in FlashList grid with strict numColumns. Selection state in a useSyncExternalStore so individual cells subscribe to their own selection number — toggling one doesn't re-render the grid.

title: Context menu: iOS 3D-Touch-style preview vs Android Material long-press
design_brief: Long-press on a list item to reveal options. iOS: scaled preview of the item + popover menu below (matching Apple's contextual menus). Android: bottom anchored menu (Material design). Reference: iOS Mail long-press, iOS Photos long-press, Android Gmail.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Identical menus on both platforms
Using Alert.alert as menu
Modal that doesn't scale-preview the source item (iOS)
Heavy blur backdrop on Android
mobile_reasoning: iOS users expect to see the source item lifted/scaled with a popover. Android users expect a bottom menu. Both triggered by long-press. iOS uses Reanimated for the preview lift + popover spring. Android branches to a Material bottom sheet menu.

title: Image preloading and viewport-aware decode for smooth feed scrolling
design_brief: A media-heavy feed that pre-decodes the next 5 images before they're visible to avoid mid-scroll decode jank. Reference: Instagram Reels prefetch, TikTok preloading, Pinterest pin loading.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No prefetch — images decode on first viewport entry
Prefetching ALL images on mount

Generic 'X' clear button (iOS has Cancel as text)
Using on Android (it's an iOS-specific convention)
mobile_reasoning: On iOS, the Cancel button appears only on focus and slides in from the right while the search bar shrinks to make room. Both transformations driven by a single 'focused' shared value via spring.

title: Edge-pull-to-trigger action (Apollo-style swipe back)
design_brief: A Reddit-Apollo-style edge swipe: pull from left or right edge of screen to trigger an action (e.g., upvote/downvote on a comment, or navigate back). A floating icon appears that scales with pull distance, locking when threshold crossed. Reference: Apollo for Reddit comment swipes, Telegram swipe-reply.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Action triggers without visual progress indicator
No haptic at threshold
Pull too lenient (false triggers)
Pull doesn't cancel cleanly if dragged back
